actions.title: Review and improve weak metadata
actions.description: Review weak or risky metadata before changing production copy or crawler signals.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Use App Router metadata exports, route-level metadata, or generateMetadata in server-rendered files.

futureAutomation.reason: Metadata changes affect production copy and crawler/share-preview signals, so generated changes should be reviewed.

actions.title: Add JSON-LD with review
actions.description: Add or fix conservative structured data only after confirming schema type and business facts.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Add a reviewed JSON-LD script or component in an App Router layout/page.

futureAutomation.reason: Structured data must be factually accurate; ShipReady should not invent schema facts without review.

actions.title: Add robots.ts
actions.description: Add a robots file or framework route so crawlers can discover the intended crawl policy.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Add an App Router robots.ts route.

futureAutomation.reason: The framework and likely robots target are clear.

actions.title: Add sitemap.ts
actions.description: Add a sitemap so crawlers can discover the public URL set.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Add an App Router sitemap.ts route. Use audited URL https://fodmapp.fr/.

futureAutomation.reason: The framework and likely sitemap target are clear.

limitations: This is a read-only plan. No files were modified and no patches were generated.
Future automation must re-check the repo before writing changes.
File writing is disabled; use `shipready fix --dry-run` for supported patch previews.
This command is read-only and does not modify repository files.
Dynamic route metadata may require human review.
Existing complex metadata logic should not be overwritten blindly.
